data.description:
## What

The test repo-isolation guard `_no_repo_root_leaks` (`tests/conftest.py:157-181`)
only diffs the **top-level** entries of `REPO_ROOT` (`set(os.listdir(_REPO_ROOT))`
before vs after each test). It is therefore **blind to a test leaking state INTO a
pre-existing top-level directory** — most importantly `.rebar/` (this checkout
dogfoods rebar, so `.rebar/` always exists locally). A test that writes
`.rebar/run_snapshots/<sha>` adds no *new* top-level entry, so the guard passes
locally — while a fresh CI checkout (no `.rebar/`) sees the SAME write *create*
`.rebar` and flags it. This is a false-green/false-red split between local and CI.

## Why

This is the exact gap that hid the `test_sweep_removes_readonly_snapshot_tree` leak
(fixed in `fc792e4f`): the test snapshotted into `cwd = REPO_ROOT`'s `.rebar/`, which
was invisible to the top-level guard locally but failed CI's clean checkout. The
guard's job is to catch tests that escape their sandbox; right now it catches only
the subset that create a *new top-level* entry, giving false confidence locally.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Ensure `REPO_ROOT/.rebar/` exists (it does in any checkout that has run rebar).
2. Add a test that writes a file under `REPO_ROOT/.rebar/` (e.g. `(_REPO_ROOT /
   ".rebar" / "leaked.txt").write_text("x")`) without using `tmp_path`.
3. Run it **locally**: the `_no_repo_root_leaks` guard does NOT fail it (the new file
   is inside the pre-existing `.rebar/`, so the top-level `os.listdir` diff is empty).
4. Run it on a **fresh checkout** (no `.rebar/`): the write creates `.rebar/` → the
   top-level diff flags `['.rebar']` → the test fails.

**Expected:** a test that writes anywhere under `REPO_ROOT` it didn't create is flagged
**locally**, regardless of whether the containing dir pre-existed.
**Actual:** leaks into a pre-existing top-level dir (`.rebar/`, etc.) pass locally and
only surface (if at all) on a clean CI checkout.

## Proposed improvement

Extend the guard to detect new entries created **inside** the volatile pre-existing
dirs, not just at the top level — while staying cheap and not flagging dirs with
legitimate per-test churn:

- Snapshot a deeper path set for the known cache/state dirs that tests must NOT touch
  in `REPO_ROOT` — at minimum `.rebar/` (one level: `{f".rebar/{e}" for e in
  os.listdir(REPO_ROOT/'.rebar')}`), diffed before/after alongside the top-level set.
  Optionally generalize to a bounded recursive walk that **excludes** the dirs with
  legitimate churn (`.git/`, `.tickets-tracker/`, `.venv/`, caches) so it stays fast.
- Keep the existing best-effort cleanup of leaked paths, extended to the deeper set.
- (Belt-and-suspenders, optional) add a CI/local parity note or a `make` target that
  runs a representative slice in a throwaway checkout, so the local↔CI environment gap
  (pre-existing `.rebar`, installed extras, filesystem) is exercised before push.

The underlying test bug is already fixed; this ticket hardens the *guard* so the next
such leak fails locally instead of only in CI.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_no_repo_root_leaks` flags a test that creates a new entry under a pre-existing
  `REPO_ROOT/.rebar/` (verified by a self-test that deliberately leaks into `.rebar/`
  and asserts the guard fails it) — even when `.rebar/` pre-exists.
- [ ] The guard still passes the full existing suite (no false positives from dirs with
  legitimate per-test churn — `.git/`, `.tickets-tracker/`, etc. remain excluded).
- [ ] The check stays inexpensive (no full-repo recursive walk of large dirs; scoped to
  the known state dirs or a bounded/excluded walk).
- [ ] Leaked deeper paths are cleaned up (best-effort) as the top-level ones already are.
</content>
data.title: test isolation guard _no_repo_root_leaks misses leaks into pre-existing top-level dirs (e.g. .rebar/)
